expectations.evidence: "One rejects → whole rejects immediately", "Use `all` → need all succeed, bail on first fail", "Use `allSettled` → need all results regardless of failures"

expectations.text: Response is noticeably more compressed than full mode would produce
expectations.evidence: Entire response is 7 lines with no code examples, no headers, no tables. Compare to the without_skill response which is ~60 lines with code blocks and a comparison table.
No contractions found in the response. The text uses "Never rejects", "waits all settle", "need all succeed" — all contraction-free.

expectations.text: The fail-fast behavior of Promise.all is explained correctly
expectations.evidence: "fail-fast. One rejects → whole rejects immediately" — correctly captures that a single rejection causes immediate rejection of the whole.

expectations.text: The status/value/reason shape of allSettled results is mentioned
expectations.evidence: "Returns `[{status, value}]` or `[{status, reason}]` per promise" — correctly identifies both the fulfilled shape (status+value) and rejected shape (status+reason).
